policy_name: Information Security Management System (ISMS) Policy v3.1

sections.heading: Security Governance and Management Direction
sections.text_summary: Board oversight, CISO accountability, Security Steering Committee, management commitment, disciplinary procedures, annual policy acknowledgement.

sections.heading: Asset Management
sections.text_summary: CMDB-based asset inventory, four-tier classification, acceptable use policy, cloud asset approval and DLP monitoring.

sections.heading: Access Control
sections.text_summary: Least privilege/need-to-know, RBAC, IAM platform, PAM with JIT elevation, semi-annual reviews, VPN for remote, session recording. MFA ABSENT (GAP 1).

sections.heading: Cryptography and Data Protection
sections.text_summary: TLS 1.2+ in transit, AES-256 at rest, disk encryption (BitLocker/FileVault), backup encryption, KMS with HSM, key rotation defined.

sections.heading: Physical and Environmental Security
sections.text_summary: Badge access perimeters, PACS logging, 90-day CCTV retention, FM-200 fire suppression, UPS/generator (72h), temperature/humidity monitoring, media sanitisation.

sections.heading: Operations Security
sections.text_summary: Anti-malware (4h update SLA), SIEM with 12-month retention, continuous SOC monitoring, Change Advisory Board for normal changes, emergency change retrospective approval. Vulnerability management ABSENT (GAP 2).

sections.heading: Incident Management
sections.text_summary: IRP maintained and tested annually, SOC as first contact, regulatory notification process, containment-eradication-recovery workflow, incident log. Incident classification criteria ABSENT (GAP 3).

sections.heading: Business Continuity and Resilience
sections.text_summary: BCP and DRP maintained, annual BIA, RTO/RPO defined per system, secondary DC for DR, semi-annual failover tests, encrypted backup. Backup FREQUENCY undefined (GAP 4).

sections.heading: Supplier and Vendor Management
sections.text_summary: Supplier security risk assessment (pre-engagement), security contractual clauses, Tier-1 annual reassessment, Approved Supplier Register. Assessment METHODOLOGY undefined (GAP 5).

sections.heading: Compliance, Audit, and Legal
sections.text_summary: Regulatory register (PDPA, MAS TRM, SOC 2), annual internal ISMS audit, software licence compliance, DPO appointed, privacy impact assessments, retention schedule.

seeded_gaps.gap_description: No multi-factor authentication (MFA) requirement specified for any access category — remote access, privileged access, or critical system access.
seeded_gaps.remediation: Section 3.4 (Remote Access) and 3.3 (Privileged Access) must be amended to require MFA for: (a) all remote VPN sessions, (b) all privileged account logins via PAM, (c) all access to Confidential/Restricted systems. Approved methods: TOTP app, hardware token, or push notification.

seeded_gaps.gap_description: No vulnerability scanning, patch management programme, or penetration testing schedule defined anywhere in the policy.
seeded_gaps.remediation: Add a new sub-section 6.4 (Vulnerability Management): (a) quarterly authenticated vulnerability scans of all internet-facing and critical internal systems, (b) patch SLAs — Critical CVEs: 24 hours, High: 7 days, Medium: 30 days, Low: 90 days, (c) annual penetration test by accredited third party, (d) results tracked in the risk register.

seeded_gaps.gap_description: Incident classification criteria are absent. There is no defined threshold or decision framework for what constitutes a 'security incident' vs. a routine 'security event.'
seeded_gaps.remediation: Add Section 7.1a (Incident Classification Criteria): Define quantitative and qualitative thresholds for declaring a security incident — including data volume thresholds, system types affected, and exposure duration. Map criteria to severity levels (P1–P4) with clear escalation matrix.

seeded_gaps.gap_description: Backup frequency is not specified for any system tier. The BCP references backup but lacks schedules, making RPO commitments unverifiable.
seeded_gaps.remediation: Amend Section 8.3 with explicit backup schedules by system tier: Tier 1 (Critical): hourly incremental, daily full; Tier 2 (Important): daily incremental, weekly full; Tier 3 (Standard): weekly full. Define RPO per tier and confirm restore tests validate these objectives.

seeded_gaps.gap_description: Third-party security assessment process is referenced but not described. No methodology, questionnaire standard (e.g., SIG, CAIQ, ISO 27001 cert check), or scoring criteria defined.
seeded_gaps.remediation: Add Section 9.1a (Supplier Assessment Methodology): Tier-1 suppliers complete the Standardised Information Gathering (SIG) questionnaire annually. Tier-2 suppliers complete a simplified 20-control checklist. Scoring criteria: <70% = assessment fail, engagement blocked pending remediation. Results stored in Approved Supplier Register.
